edges.description: Impaired triglyceride hydrolysis causes triglyceride deposition in heart and vessel cells.

nodes.description: Undegraded triglyceride accumulates in cardiomyocytes and coronary vascular cells, impairing cardiac energy metabolism and driving cardiomyopathy and coronary artery disease.

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
These patients suffered
from intractable heart failure requiring cardiac transplantation

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
TGCV with and without PNPLA2 mutations were designated as primary TGCV (P-TGCV) and idiopathic
TGCV (I-TGCV), respectively

nodes.description: Biallelic PNPLA2 variants reduce adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL), which catalyzes the rate-limiting intracellular hydrolysis of triglyceride to release long-chain fatty acids as a major energy source.

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy (TGCV) is a phenotype primarily
reported in patients carrying genetic mutations in PNPLA2 encoding adipose
triglyceride lipase (ATGL) which releases long chain fatty acid (LCFA) as a
major energy source by the intracellular TG hydrolysis.
